title: Deployment Strategist
key_evidence: Scope: AI-native data collection / agents for the Testing, Inspection & Certification (TIC) sector — automates inspector admin, claims ~50% capacity unlock / up to 45% faster cycle times. Founded 2024 in London, early-stage (pre-seed/seed), small team. Founder/CEO Jonathan Low.

Role explicitly framed as EARLY-CAREER Deployment Strategist: scale post-sales processes, grow large enterprise accounts, operate at intersection of business and product, translate customer needs into roadmap priorities. Early-career framing favours Ryan's level (vs senior FDE roles).
Salary £80K-100K + equity (per ZipRecruiter/Jooble listing via Jack & Jill ATS) — comfortably above £50k floor; strong conversion signal.
Small 2024 startup = founder-led hiring, high human-read probability; lower prominence than ElevenLabs/Decagon = lower applicant volume = favourable competition.

Spike fit moderate: enterprise/post-sales deployment in TIC (industrial inspection) is off-domain (not crypto/AI-consumer), and leans toward enterprise account growth which Ryan lacks; BUT 'early-career', 'translate customer needs into roadmap', business+product intersection suits a generalist operator, and Claude-Code/agent literacy is relevant.
No explicit hard Python gate found (unlike ElevenLabs) — described as business/product role, lowering the SWE blocker.
Warm-path: small London team, founder active on LinkedIn, applies via Jack & Jill external ATS — discoverable, founder likely reviews.
biggest_lever: Early-career + business/product framing + no hard SWE gate + small founder-led team means Ryan's founding-generalist + Claude-Code agent literacy + stakeholder-facing skills can land a human read at a reachable seniority.
biggest_risk: TIC/industrial-inspection domain is far from crypto/AI-consumer and the role wants enterprise post-sales/account-growth experience Ryan doesn't have; domain ramp + lack of B2B delivery track record.
apply_verdict: Apply — best balanced bet of the deployment-strategist roles: reachable seniority, good pay, founder-read, no Python gate, despite off-domain.
recency_note: Ashby publishedAt 2026-01-14 (~21 weeks / 146d old) — stale, >12wk bracket (G=10). No updatedAt/repost, no applicant count. Long-lived early-stage req; recency is the dominant drag, callback 62 -> 49.
momentum_signals: STRONG FUNDING MOMENTUM: $20M / EUR17.3M Series A led by Index Ventures (w/ Susa, Entrepreneurs First, ex-DeepMind/MongoDB/Omio angels) announced ~2026-05-19, EXPLICITLY 'to grow its London-based team' — major positive, ~3wk old (Sifted/EU-Startups/Startup.eu).

ACTIVE TEAM SCALE: 6 open Ashby roles, 5 of them fresh post-raise — Deployment Lead (Mar 30), 2x Software Engineer (Apr 9), Forward Deployed Engineer (Apr 29), Product Operations (May 25). Clear hiring spike following the raise.

CAVEAT on THIS seat: Deployment Strategist (posted 2026-01-14, ~146d) is the OLDEST req and a more-senior 'Deployment Lead' (Mar 30) was added after — risk the Strategist seat is being superseded/absorbed by a senior hire. Company momentum strong, this specific req is the stale one.
NO NEGATIVE SIGNALS: founded 2024, founder Jonathan Low active; TIC tailwind (40% of technicians retiring in 5-10yr). Company-level momentum unambiguously green.
